Title: Reddit - r/stanford (Thread: Taking food out the dining hall)

Reviewer: ineedfundingpls
Date: 2 months ago
Review: Yes, you're allowed to take plates outside (since there's outdoor seating too), though you are expected to return the plates/cutlery. It's buffet style, so you can come back inside and get seconds, thirds, etc. You used to be able to take containers out, but that was phased ou

thirds, etc. You used to be able to take containers out, but that was phased out a few years ago (theoretically speaking, you could still take plates out and then pour them into containers). If you're worried about cost, I found that groceries are much cheaper, but then you have to cook, clean, etc etc. The Trader Joe's nearby has decent prices.

Reviewer: moonlike1245
Date: 2 months ago
Review:

's nearby has decent prices.

Reviewer: moonlike1245
Date: 2 months ago
Review: Theoretically speaking, you could just pour food into containers inside the dining halls 🤫

Reviewer: Stanford_experiencer
Date: 2 months ago
Review: If you're worried about cost, I found that groceries are much cheaper. IIRC, like other universities, undergrads are forced to buy "dining dollars". I sometimes wrap a bu

iversities, undergrads are forced to buy "dining dollars". I sometimes wrap a burger or two (w/bun but no wet toppings) in a few napkins and carry it out in front of everyone and no one cares. Just don't start dragging out gallon ziploc bags of food. Also, if you have your own insulated water bottle, you can fill it with coffee/milk/juice to take with you, and that's also reasonable.

Reviewer: sd

th coffee/milk/juice to take with you, and that's also reasonable.

Reviewer: sdbabygirl97
Date: 2 months ago
Review: yep. food to plate to tupperware. but tbf the food is not my favorite so i didnt rly keep doing this. i did however take 6 apples, bananas, oranges, whatever fruit as i left the dining hall lmao also for grad students you can buy dining “blocks” or something. like 20 breakfast, 20

r grad students you can buy dining “blocks” or something. like 20 breakfast, 20 lunch, 20 dinner or smth like that. (its been a min for me)

Reviewer: Personal_Dot_7196
Date: 2 months ago
Review: Do a lot of grad students pay for meal plans? I thought grad students just make food at home since groceries are cheaper or occasionally go out.

Reviewer: LetterheadClassic306
Date: 2 months ago
Review:

occasionally go out.

Reviewer: LetterheadClassic306
Date: 2 months ago
Review: ngl i did this all the time as a grad student. plates are fine but staff might side-eye you if it's obvious. what helped me was grabbing a few reusable containers - way less awkward and you can pack real meals. dining halls don't officially love takeout but no one stopped me when i used my own stuff. honestly worth hav

love takeout but no one stopped me when i used my own stuff. honestly worth having for late nights in the library anyway.

Reviewer: Cautious_Gap3645
Date: 2 months ago
Review: If the intention here is to take food for later meals, that's not cool. This raises prices for everyone. If the intention is to eat outside, that's fine.

Reviewer: giegoods
Date: 2 months ago
Review: i'm an undergrad and

hat's fine.

Reviewer: giegoods
Date: 2 months ago
Review: i'm an undergrad and i live near a dining hall but i just grab food and bring it back to my room and eat- no one will question or stop you!

Review: A lot of people around me complain but I actually like it. Probably because I only ate here for a quarter and ate at Wilbur the best dining hall most of the time.

Reviewer: hicksie51
Date: 4 years ago
Review: It's cool. It's not five stars but it's decent. Think hotel continental b

eview: It's cool. It's not five stars but it's decent. Think hotel continental breakfast. With the same energy for lunch and dinner. There is always a salad bar and they do burgers every so often. There's usually a pasta option and some chicken dish 4-5 nights out of the week. You can look at the menus online. The only day I saw available to view was next Saturday they're having kalua pork with gr

day I saw available to view was next Saturday they're having kalua pork with green curry which is honestly my favorite meal they serve.

t's quite good! Some meals/dining halls better than others but generally nothing to complain about.

Reviewer: FinalPush

Review: Wilbur dinner specials are the best, though you'll have to stomach 20-40 min wait times and the possibility of the food running out like an hour before closing. Llaga is consistent, lakeside has late night which is both purchasable with dining dollars and quite cheap for stanford food. Stern and Llaga rep

sable with dining dollars and quite cheap for stanford food. Stern and Llaga reportedly have good tater tots for weekend brunch, though they've both been letting me down immensely in recent memory. You just can't win sometimes.

Reviewer: TheYellowMamba06
Date: 3 years ago
Review: Weird prospective student energy.

The Definitive Ranking of Stanford Dining Halls
April 1, 2015

Spoon University
It’s 6 pm, you just came home from a full day of classes, finished a physics review session, and are on your way to orchestra practice. On top of all that, you’re hungry. Where do you go?

Dining halls are notorious for having bad food—some worse than others. This is the guide to all things Stanford dining—where you sh

worse than others. This is the guide to all things Stanford dining—where you should go when, where to avoid, which dining halls have specialties, and more.

And without further ado, the definitive ranking of Stanford dining halls:

1. Florence Moore Dining
dining hall
Photo by Tamara Mekler

Hand-scooped ice cream everyday and phenomenal Indian food on Sunday nights? FloMo is a campus favorite ac

ryday and phenomenal Indian food on Sunday nights? FloMo is a campus favorite across the boards.

2. Manzanita Dining
dining hall
Photo by Tamara Mekler

Manz features frozen yogurt, grilled selections, and pizza every day. All your favorites in one dining hall—eat your heart out.

3. Ricker Dining
dining hall
Photo by Tamara Mekler

Four words: “Death by Chocolate Cake.” If that doesn’t make you

Tamara Mekler

Four words: “Death by Chocolate Cake.” If that doesn’t make you immediate flock to Ricker on Tuesdays for lunch and Thursdays for dinner, maybe its signature honey biscuits will.

4. Branner Dining
dining hall
Photo by Tamara Mekler

Nothing exceptional about Branner but extensive vegan and vegetarian options, homemade soups, and consistently good food five nights a week makes it a

ptions, homemade soups, and consistently good food five nights a week makes it a go-to dining hall.

5. Stern Dining
dining hall
Photo by Tamara Mekler

Chipotle-style burrito bowls save an otherwise ho-hum dining hall. This reporter also loves the berry bars. Make sure to get there early because the lines get really long, really quickly.

6. Lakeside Dining
dining hall
Photo by Tamara Mekler

If

ong, really quickly.

6. Lakeside Dining
dining hall
Photo by Tamara Mekler

If you haven’t gone to Late Night @ Lag and ordered a piping hot churro, you haven’t had the full Stanford experience.

7. Wilbur Dining
dining hall
Photo by Tamara Mekler

Wilbs, an Asian-themed dining hall, has two redeeming qualities: Mongolian Noodles on Monday night and brunch with DIY smoothies on Saturday and Sunda

lian Noodles on Monday night and brunch with DIY smoothies on Saturday and Sunday. Also, the paninis are to die for.

8. Arrillaga Family Dining Commons
dining hall
Photo by Tamara Mekler

The Arrillaga chicken is notorious for being as dry as a desert… that being said, Arrillaga consistently serves healthy food and hey, if that’s not your style, they have a kick-butt late night option as well.
y, if that’s not your style, they have a kick-butt late night option as well.

Stanford students on their favorite dining halls
The front of Wilbur Dining Hall
(Photo: Stanford Daily file photo)
By Vienna Du
Jan. 9, 2022, 9:31 p.m.
Stanford’s 12 residential community-based dining halls have many perks such as the vibrant Chicano murals in Stern Dining, the flavorful pho and hearty Thai curries served at Wilbur and the rich desserts at Arrillaga. Many students have strong opi

rved at Wilbur and the rich desserts at Arrillaga. Many students have strong opinions regarding which dining halls they like or dislike, and why they go to each. 

Vikram Sivashankar ’25 described the food at the Arrillaga Family Dining Commons as “kind of miserable.” 

 Epstein Files

“It has some form of bland chicken every single day,” he said, quipping that there’s “a widely-held opinion that

n every single day,” he said, quipping that there’s “a widely-held opinion that if you eat at Arrillaga every single day, by the end of the first quarter you’re basically 70% chicken and 30% human!” 

But Sivashankar said that Arrillaga’s bright spot is the desserts. Indeed, Arrillaga often has a wide array of sweet selections, including chocolate and mango cakes, soft serve with toppings, donuts

lections, including chocolate and mango cakes, soft serve with toppings, donuts and cookies.

For some students, though, the decision to visit a particular dining hall goes beyond just the food selection, according to Edward Huang ’24. 

“If I’m busy with homework or there’s a deadline coming up and I need a quick meal, I’ll go to Arrillaga,” Huang said. “If I want a quiet, peaceful meal I’ll go t

, I’ll go to Arrillaga,” Huang said. “If I want a quiet, peaceful meal I’ll go to Branner.” 

Wilbur Dining, on the other hand, offers Huang a more sentimental experience because it is where he goes to “feel nostalgic for the summer because that was a really fun time to eat there.”

Yet the highly-contested question remains: which dining hall do students like the best? 

“Stern is my favorite dini

mains: which dining hall do students like the best? 

“Stern is my favorite dining hall,” Sivashankar said, who enjoys the burrito bowl station. 

Lily Raaka ’24 said that her neighborhood’s dining hall, Gerhard Casper Dining Commons, has the “most amazing roasted vegetables” and “definitely has the best fish,” which sets it apart from other dining halls. 

“There’s not really a time that Casper h

ets it apart from other dining halls. 

“There’s not really a time that Casper has disappointed me,” Raaka said. “It’s always a solid option.” 

While Sivashankar and Raaka both had a favorite dining hall that came to mind, Huang said that the quality of the food at each of the dining halls is comparable.

Sivashankar recommended following the Stanford Dining Instagram page to identify which dinin

recommended following the Stanford Dining Instagram page to identify which dining hall to visit each night. For instance, Sivashankar said that Stanford Dining had previously posted about risotto being served at a dining hall.

“You know which dining hall to tackle based on which post looks good,” Sivashankar said.

Stanford Dining has come a long way since the University first opened in 1891, whe

anford Dining has come a long way since the University first opened in 1891, when meals were almost entirely carbohydrate-based and the dining halls were segregated by gender. Now, a variety of fresh food is served daily, and sustainability is emphasized through efforts to reduce food waste and implement plant-forward menus.
menus.

Dear Stanford students, parents and community,
 

Welcome to Stanford, we are so pleased that you are here!

R&DE Stanford Dining, Hospitality & Auxiliaries (SDHA), a division of Residential & Dining Enterprises, is a best-in-class dining and hospitality program serving over 25,000 meals a day across dining halls, row houses, co-ops, suites, independent houses, branded concepts, athletic dining, a

houses, co-ops, suites, independent houses, branded concepts, athletic dining, a kosher kitchen, late night dining, cafes, retail programs, markets, a sweet shop, catering operations, Schwab Executive Dining, and a teaching kitchen. SDHA also oversees the Stanford Food Institute, the Stanford Food Pantry, and a staff training and development center. We are excited to nourish you with delicious, he

raining and development center. We are excited to nourish you with delicious, healthy and sustainable food!

It is clear in feedback from students that conversations and making connections over meals form the center of their communities. When students break bread together, they engage in intellectual and lively discussions with each other, faculty, and other members of the Stanford community. This

ions with each other, faculty, and other members of the Stanford community. This is an essential part of the Stanford residential education experience. Stanford strongly believes in community building over meals, and R&DE SDHA is committed to ensuring this happens through the meal plan program. These options allow students, faculty, staff, and their partners the ease and flexibility of eating thro

ents, faculty, staff, and their partners the ease and flexibility of eating throughout campus and nourishes the development of friendships across the university.

We pride ourselves on providing high-quality and nutritious cuisine to meet the wide variety of dietary needs within the Stanford community. The Eat Well @Stanford program is committed to supporting an inclusive dining experience for stu

anford program is committed to supporting an inclusive dining experience for students with a wide range of dietary needs, and enhancing the development of lifelong eating practices that promote health and wellbeing. New this year, Branner Dining is a dedicated allergen-friendly dining hall and all meals will be free of the top nine allergens. Students with nut sensitivities are encouraged to conti

the top nine allergens. Students with nut sensitivities are encouraged to continue to visit Stern Dining and Ricker Dining. Students with religious requirements considerations including halal or kosher meals, can receive assistance in the dining halls by contacting nutritionist@stanford.edu. Dining hall managers and chefs are always available during meal service to help you navigate food options.

hefs are always available during meal service to help you navigate food options. 

Our chefs are dedicated to inspiring students with delicious flavors, enticing recipes and expertly crafted meals. We believe that food builds community and we often introduce students to new foods, global cuisines, and food experiences which supports the academic mission. We integrate culturally inspired food conce

which supports the academic mission. We integrate culturally inspired food concepts and culinary celebrations into the meal plan program. Learn more about our culinary team, what they are cooking, and nightly specials by following us on Instagram @StanfordDining!

We also want you to get directly involved! Open to all undergraduate students currently residing in the dorms, the Student Dining Commi

undergraduate students currently residing in the dorms, the Student Dining Committee is a great way to get involved with our team and share ideas to enhance the dining experience for all students. We also hire students. We offer flexible schedules and minimal shifts that work around your school schedule. Have questions or feedback for your dining hall chef or manager? We hold weekly dining hall of

or feedback for your dining hall chef or manager? We hold weekly dining hall office hours where you can speak directly to our team. To learn more about these programs, please visit our Student Engagement webpage.

Sustainability is one of our core values, and we are committed to pursuing bold action to address climate change and support Stanford’s commitment to become a zero-waste campus by 2035.

change and support Stanford’s commitment to become a zero-waste campus by 2035. Our focus with our One Plate, One Planet program is to enhance and create a more impactful student dining experience for the health of our students and for the good of our planet.

We recognize the importance and essential role that delicious and nutritious meals play in building community. It is our hope that our din

ous and nutritious meals play in building community. It is our hope that our dining program will help create an exceptional experience this year. Please don't hesitate to reach out if there's anything we can do to support you. You can reach our staff at diningplans@stanford.edu or leave us a comment, question or concern using our feedback form.

We look forward to a wonderful year ahead!

n using our feedback form.

We look forward to a wonderful year ahead!

Dining Hall Name: Arrillaga Family Dining Commons
Address: 489 Arguello Way, Stanford, CA 94305
Overall Rating: 4.3
Total Reviews: 12

Reviewer: Ting L.
Date: 2 months ago
Review: Had a great experience at Arrillaga Family Dining Commons. The space was clean, bright, and comfortable, and there was a nice variety of options to choose from. I especially enjoyed the fresh salad bar with lots of color

ions to choose from. I especially enjoyed the fresh salad bar with lots of colorful ingredients, and the smoked salmon plate with avocado and fries was simple but satisfying. The coffee and dessert were also a nice finish to the meal. Overall, the food felt fresh, the ambiance was pleasant, and it was an enjoyable dining experience. The cashier was very attentive and friendly.

Reviewer: Mona A.
D

ng experience. The cashier was very attentive and friendly.

Reviewer: Mona A.
Date: 2 months ago
Review: I had the stinkiest burger of the past year here at their late night tonight. It tasted like a ten year old carcass grilled in gasoline. Sucks too cause I had the best buzz going from herbal tea tonight. This campus has a knack for always ruining the vibes.

Reviewer: Sarae S.
Date: 3 years ag

s has a knack for always ruining the vibes.

Reviewer: Sarae S.
Date: 3 years ago
Review: The gyros were stellar! The pita was soft and the meat was flavorful. The desert(donut), however, tasted store bought. I was pleasantly surprised Arrillaga is not known for being the best dining hall on campus, but they definitely wowed me with this one

Reviewer: Vivian C.
Date: 6 years ago
Review: COVID 19

wowed me with this one

Reviewer: Vivian C.
Date: 6 years ago
Review: COVID 19 REVIEW This place is keeping me alive on campus. Take out only! WEAR A MASK, you go in, you scan your card, you check your temperature (with a call camera scan thing), wash your hands, you get a paper bag, you fill up your bag, you get out. You can take whatever fits in the bag! Everything is prepackaged in single serv

You can take whatever fits in the bag! Everything is prepackaged in single serving containers. One direction traffic. Once you move on you can't turn back! What they always have: -side salads, hummus, tuna salad (these have the packaging date! A+) -yogurt (whole milk, clover greek yogurt) -pastries (cookies, cakes, croissants, not the best, but will hit the spot if you're craving it meh) (these h

oissants, not the best, but will hit the spot if you're craving it meh) (these have the packaging date! A+) -packaged bowls of cereal -whole fruit (bananas, apples, etc) -packaged cheese (like string cheese and cubes of cheddar) -boxes of cut fruit: its a mix of strawberries, blueberries, honeydew, cantaloupe, pineapple, grapes (these have the packaging date! A+) -bottled drinks (bottled water, Ho

e, grapes (these have the packaging date! A+) -bottled drinks (bottled water, Honest Tea, Gatorade, orange juice) -generic ice cream (choco tacos, ice cream sandwiches) -condiments, chips, packaged cookies, teas What rotates: -chicken entree -vegetarian entree -allergy friendly entree -soup option: irish bacon stew, chicken tortilla, tomato basil -pasta option: cheese ravioli, pasta bolognese, pas

icken tortilla, tomato basil -pasta option: cheese ravioli, pasta bolognese, pasta marinara, etc What they OCCASIONALLY have that are special treats!: - small cartons of blue bottle coffee - haagen dazs ice cream bars - beef burgers sometimes with cheese (this is a decent burger) Wednesdays are the best in my opinion: loco moco, huli huli Chicken, pork ramen, chimichurri steak! Chimichurri steak:

loco moco, huli huli Chicken, pork ramen, chimichurri steak! Chimichurri steak: They only let you take one entree of this one because it is a legit piece of steak done medium rare to medium and I am so impressed! They definitely use some meat tenderizer because this steak is TENDER! Loco moco: A delicious beef burger patty with gravy and rice and veggies! So good! It definitely is healthier than t

atty with gravy and rice and veggies! So good! It definitely is healthier than the loco moco you will get in Hawaii, but it is delicious! I wish they added more gravy though! Chicken/Vegetarian Broth Pho: This was delicious! but such a hassle to pack! because the noodles come in one bowl, the chicken comes in another, and the soup in another bowl, so it takes up a lot of space. Sometimes they will

and the soup in another bowl, so it takes up a lot of space. Sometimes they will have specials that are not mentioned on the menu: Pork Ramen, Kao Soi Noodles, loaded baked potato, Korean seafood pancake! Such delightful surprises! These definitely make my day, but they don't fit well in the bag lol.

Reviewer: Jasper K.
Date: 1 year ago
Review: i love arrillaga family dining commons, thank you mr

K.
Date: 1 year ago
Review: i love arrillaga family dining commons, thank you mr. arrillaga and your big big wallet. the pizza is gas, the salad bar is extensive, and the service is even better. live love llaga.

Reviewer: Sean Y.
Date: 3 years ago
Review: Admittedly, I haven't been to all the dining halls on campus. However, it is substantially nicer than Lakeside or Wilbur. The food here is fres

owever, it is substantially nicer than Lakeside or Wilbur. The food here is fresh, plentiful, and delicious.

Reviewer: Leee D.
Date: 3 years ago
Review: Arrillaga is one of the best cafeterias at Stanford (it's located at the heart of campus), and with its wide variety of quality food options, I can always find food that I enjoy (or I can customize for what I'm feeling that day)! Also, it goes wi

hat I enjoy (or I can customize for what I'm feeling that day)! Also, it goes without saying that the environment at Arrillaga (and Stanford in general) is beautiful, so it's a great place to dine. Staff are also always very friendly :)

Reviewer: Ashlee A.
Date: 7 years ago
Review: Beautiful dining hall with balcony seating outside as well. A lot of dining options and flavored water as well. Love

eating outside as well. A lot of dining options and flavored water as well. Love the vegetarian options. Ice cream machine. This was from a summer stay experience.

Reviewer: Dave Y.
Date: 7 years ago
Review: Frankly, I've had better. Call it the Stanford halo effect (or whatever you want to call it), but my expectations were extremely high. I expected stations upon stations (like Cornell or UCLA)

ns were extremely high. I expected stations upon stations (like Cornell or UCLA), but there are really only three categories of stations--salad bar, pizza, and entree of the day. The first time can be blissful. The crowd and the bustle easily deceives you into thinking that there's a lot of action, argo food-action, going on. But after the second and third day, you begin catch onto the pattern. Th

ing on. But after the second and third day, you begin catch onto the pattern. They really don't have much diverse options and offerings. Our summer program used this particular dining hall for lunches. I hear that the food is better during the school year. So this ain't me throwing shade at Stanford dining in general; it's me throwing shade at their summer dining. Go to Tresidder instead.

Reviewe

it's me throwing shade at their summer dining. Go to Tresidder instead.

Reviewer: Rosa Y.
Date: 8 years ago
Review: I'm a fan. Unlimited coffee, diverse + tasty food options, affordable prices, relaxed with great lighting. Definitely beats my college dining hall. On weekends, brunch is a great value and there are plenty of Stanford families around!

Reviewer: Spencer S.
Date: 4 years ago
Review:

ty of Stanford families around!

Reviewer: Spencer S.
Date: 4 years ago
Review: Good diversity of options and I can always find some food I'll be happy with. Definitely great for a dining hall. They always have huge bowls for cereal which is essential. Bonus points because Chef Tami is a homie!

Reviewer: Stephen S.
Date: 7 years ago
Review: Cafeteria quality food at an overrated price. Only worth

te: 7 years ago
Review: Cafeteria quality food at an overrated price. Only worth if you mistakenly bought meal plan.

Dining Hall Name: Arrillaga Nights
Address: 489 Arguello Way, Stanford, CA 94305
Overall Rating: 3.4
Total Reviews: 22

Reviewer: Justin W.
Date: 1 year ago
Review: Arrillaga Nights is literally just the normal dining hall but with slightly longer lines and greasy food at 11 PM. It's nice that there is a late-night option on this side of campus since walking all the way to Lakeside or Axe & Palm g

ion on this side of campus since walking all the way to Lakeside or Axe & Palm gets old. The mozzarella sticks and fries are usually warm, but nothing to write home about.

Reviewer: Maya L.
Date: 2 years ago
Review: The burgers here hit the spot after a long study session, but the wait times can get absolutely ridiculous. Sometimes you're standing around for 20 minutes just for a basket of chicke

ous. Sometimes you're standing around for 20 minutes just for a basket of chicken tenders. It's a lifesaver when you're starving late at night, but definitely plan ahead or go during off-peak times.

Reviewer: David K.
Date: 3 years ago
Review: Classic late night Stanford vibes. The food is pretty greasy and definitely overpriced for the quality, but when your dining dollars are burning a hole in

overpriced for the quality, but when your dining dollars are burning a hole in your pocket and it's midnight, you don't have many other choices. The staff is usually pretty tired but friendly enough given the chaos.

Reviewer: Chloe M.
Date: 3 years ago
Review: Pretty mediocre. Half the time the food tastes like it's been sitting under a heat lamp for an hour, and the menu rarely changes. They ne

been sitting under a heat lamp for an hour, and the menu rarely changes. They need more vegetarian options for late night besides just basic fries or a sad grilled cheese.

Reviewer: Brandon T.
Date: 4 years ago
Review: Love the convenience of having late-night food right near the dorms. The breakfast burritos they sometimes do are awesome. It gets crowded fast, especially on Thursday nights, but
etimes do are awesome. It gets crowded fast, especially on Thursday nights, but it's a staple of the campus experience.

Dining Hall Name: Stern Dining Hall
Address: 618 Escondido Road, Stanford, CA 94305
Overall Rating: 3.3
Total Reviews: 7

Reviewer: Tamara M.
Date: 2 years ago
Review: Chipotle-style burrito bowls save an otherwise ho-hum dining hall. This reporter also loves the berry bars. Make sure to get there early because the lines get really long, really quickly.

Reviewer: Kevin L.
Date: 3 years ago
Review

es get really long, really quickly.

Reviewer: Kevin L.
Date: 3 years ago
Review: Stern focuses mostly on Latin American and Mexican food. Their taco and burrito bar is the main attraction here. The line moves decently fast but can back up heavily right at noon during the lunch rush. Rice, beans, and meat options are solid, and the guacamole is actually pretty good for a dining hall.

Reviewer: Sa

olid, and the guacamole is actually pretty good for a dining hall.

Reviewer: Sarah W.
Date: 4 years ago
Review: Best brunch on the weekends if you like breakfast burritos. During the week it gets pretty repetitive since it's almost always Tex-Mex style food at the main station, but the salad bar has good fresh greens.

Reviewer: Jason T.
Date: 5 years ago
Review: Decent food but the seating area

Reviewer: Jason T.
Date: 5 years ago
Review: Decent food but the seating area can get loud and cramped since it's connected directly to the freshman dorms. The quesadillas are hit or miss depending on who is working the grill line that day.

Reviewer: Michael S.
Date: 5 years ago
Review: If you want a quick burrito or taco bowl, it works out fine. It's not top tier campus dining like Arrillaga b

r taco bowl, it works out fine. It's not top tier campus dining like Arrillaga but it completely beats out Wilbur on its bad days.

Reviewer: Emily R.
Date: 6 years ago
Review: Standard cafeteria vibes. Love the fresh salsa selections and the option to load up on fajita veggies. Wish they changed up the main entrees more often though.

Reviewer: Brandon C.
Date: 7 years ago
Review: A bit overrated

re often though.

Reviewer: Brandon C.
Date: 7 years ago
Review: A bit overrated if you aren't in the mood for Mexican food, as the other stations are quite small. It gets the job done if you live in Stern Hall and don't want to walk in the rain.

oop ice cream nights. It can get super packed on those specific weekdays, but it is totally worth it.

ders, and customizable breakfast sandwiches. The wait times can occasionally balloon past midnight, but the staff does their best.

Reviewer: Andrew A.
Date: 3 months ago
Review: Some old reviews. Food is good. Not sure why a review is allowed from 10 years ago. Just note the weekend has a normal menu.

Reviewer: Ray G.
Date: 3 years ago
Review: Wilbur dining is one of the most impressive dining halls I've ever e

ago
Review: Wilbur dining is one of the most impressive dining halls I've ever eaten at. The Star Ginger team is a class of its own, serving an impressive variety of Asian dishes. In particular, Sam is one of the most talented chefs I've ever met. His complete mastery of making chashao is a gift to this campus. I've eaten chashao at hundreds and hundreds of restaurants and food stalls from Hong Ko

ten chashao at hundreds and hundreds of restaurants and food stalls from Hong Kong and New York, and Sam's art of marinating and cooking chashao is unparalleled. Bravo!!!

Reviewer: William B.
Date: 14 years ago
Review: So it is dorm food, but it sure is better than the dorm food they had when I was in college. Whether that's a consequence of ten years of escalation in university amenities, or a c

that's a consequence of ten years of escalation in university amenities, or a consequence of being located in California, I do not know. The best things are the pho bar and the banh mi bar. I like that they let you pile your own veggies onto the banh mi; I dislike that they don't let you pile your own veggies into the pho. It's also all-you-can-eat, if that's something you're into.

Review: Extremely hit or miss. Been having lots of bad food recently from here. My complaint especially is about not having any meat options at all. At times there's only some disgusting curry which looks extremely appetizing and white jasmine rice. After which everyone heads to the grill, and y

ppetizing and white jasmine rice. After which everyone heads to the grill, and you have to wait 10 minutes to get your burger. I used to prefer Wilbur over Stern but I've recently changed my mind.

o think that vegetarians only have the following edible options: -broccoli -cauliflower -whole wheat undercooked pasta -drinks However, several people seem to love Wilbur. It could be because they have edible options other than what I listed above. The only think I particularly enjoyed was the smoothie I could make only twice a week during brunch. The only fruits they have are strawberries, mangoe

y twice a week during brunch. The only fruits they have are strawberries, mangoes, some berries, and bananas (thankfully I appreciate these). Long story short, I will not return to Wilbur unless I'm really craving a smoothie that I don't want to pay for at Jamba Juice.
